channel.title: Robert Boles
channel.description: A practical knowledge base from Robert Boles on QA systems, automation, release confidence, and builder experiments.

item.title: How I Triage Flaky Automation
item.description: A repeatable way to classify flaky tests by cause before you touch a single line of test code.

item.title: How To Structure A Selenium Test
item.description: A layered structure that keeps UI tests readable and survivable when the interface changes.

item.title: How To Build An API Smoke Suite
item.description: A small, fast, stable suite that decides whether a build is even worth deeper testing.

item.title: How To Write A Useful Regression Report
item.description: A report shape that leads with the decision and the blockers instead of a wall of pass/fail counts.

item.title: How To Write A Test Plan That Does Not Become Junk
item.description: A short, decision-oriented test plan that captures risk and scope instead of describing everything.

item.title: A Simple QA Dashboard That Leaders Actually Read
item.description: A dashboard that puts the release decision first and treats charts as supporting evidence.

item.title: A Better Handoff Template For Long-Running Projects
item.description: A handoff structure that transfers decisions and context, not just files and repositories.

item.title: Turning Side Projects Into Proof Of Engineering Judgment
item.description: How to make a side project demonstrate judgment and tradeoffs, not just that you can finish things.

item.title: What Good QA Actually Owns
item.description: A practical breakdown of what strong QA ownership looks like beyond clicking through tickets and counting defects.

item.title: Regression Pass Rate Is Not Release Confidence
item.description: Why pass rate is useful but dangerous when treated as the main signal for release readiness.

item.title: My Practical Release Readiness Checklist
item.description: A field-ready checklist for deciding whether a release is actually understood, not merely finished.

item.title: How I Review A Failed Nightly Automation Run
item.description: A repeatable triage pattern for making failed automation runs useful instead of noisy.

item.title: When Not To Automate A Test
item.description: A practical filter for deciding whether automation will increase confidence or just add maintenance drag.

item.title: Test Data Is Usually The Real E2E Problem
item.description: Why E2E testing often fails because of data control, not because the test idea is wrong.

item.title: What Vox Mana Taught Me About Data Contracts
item.description: How a side project reinforced the value of source layers, generated data boundaries, and explicit contracts.

item.title: How I Use AI/Codex Without Losing Control Of The Project
item.description: A practical workflow for using AI assistants while keeping ownership, scope, and decision history clear.
